description_long: This is a large, circular stone maze with thick, low walls constructed from rough-hewn stone blocks. The maze features a single, clearly defined entrance on the outer edge, leading into a series of concentric pathways that spiral toward the center. The walls are uniform in height and thickness, giving the structure a robust and ancient appearance. The stonework is consistent throughout, with a natural, unpolished texture that suggests durability and age. There is no overhead coverage, leaving the entire maze open to the sky. The overall design is reminiscent of classical or medieval stone labyrinths often found in public parks or historical sites.
description: A circular stone maze with thick, rough-hewn walls and a single entrance, featuring concentric pathways and an open top. The structure has a robust, ancient appearance and is fully exposed to the sky.
description_view_0: The front view shows the main entrance to the maze, with the stone walls and concentric paths clearly visible.
description_view_1: This side view highlights the uniform height and thickness of the stone walls, with the entrance less visible.
description_view_2: Another angled view showing the maze's circular form and the intricate pattern of the internal pathways.
description_view_3: A rear view displaying the continuous stone wall and the maze's open, spiraling layout without a visible entrance.
attribute_entry_type: single entrance located on the outer edge, no distinctive gate or archway visible
attribute_wall_pattern: walls are constructed from rough, irregular stone blocks with a consistent, unpolished texture and no decorative motifs
attribute_path_complexity: moderate; the maze features concentric paths with some branching and dead ends, but the layout is not highly intricate
attribute_overhead_coverage: fully open to the sky, no overhead coverage or roofing present
